expectations.text: the agent should read skills/crafting-skills/SKILL.md OR invoke the Skill tool on crafting-skills before drafting the new skill's frontmatter
the new SKILL.md body should start with a Decision Router / Hub Contract section that enumerates sibling skills and routes to them by intent
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the output file parses as a valid SKILL.md with name + description + license frontmatter, and the body markdown has at least one actionable section
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the new skill's frontmatter description should follow the Compendium's 'Load when… / Use when… / Do NOT use for… (use Y)' structure with concrete trigger phrases, not a generic capability statement
expectations.evidence: The agent did not produce any new skill file; transcript shows only an initial query without generating a skill, so the required frontmatter structure is absent.

expectations.text: the new skill should not be a verbatim recipe copy of an existing skill (e.g. 'follow the PDF parsing tutorial'); it should encode project-specific guidance the base agent lacks
expectations.evidence: No skill was generated, therefore no assessment can be made that it avoids being a verbatim copy; as no skill exists, the assertion fails.
